pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Charles Comte — Traité de Législation (Volumes I-IV)
series_anchor.palette: lead grey, ivory, ink black, oxidized brass, considered neutrals
series_anchor.mood: 18th-century European salon at lamplight. Restrained, rational. The philosopher's writing desk after the household has gone to bed.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Cool dry washes with controlled pigment. Allow paper grain to dominate in the negative space. A single warm note (oxidized brass or candle warmth) introduced sparingly to break the cool palette.

1_context.content_excerpt:
## CHAP. 1: CHAPTER ONE.

> Of the various ways of treating the moral and legislative sciences; of the phenomena necessary to know in order to master these sciences; of the causes that have arrested or favored their progress; and of the object of this work.

In writing on legislation, my goal is not to present a system of laws, nor to attack or defend the institutions of any particular country; I wish simply to investigate the causes that make a people prosper or decline, or that render it stationary. To devote myself to these investigations, I need neither to imagine systems nor to reason fro

output.reasoning: Comte's opening declares his method: empirical investigation of causes behind social prosperity or decline, not system-building. A single beam scale in oxidized brass evokes the rational weighing of causes — restrained, Enlightenment, legislative — without depicting a courtroom or human figure. It anchors the series mood of lamplight rationalism at a writing desk.
output.subject: A single oxidized brass balance scale, one pan slightly lower than the other
output.palette: Payne's grey, oxidized brass (yellow ochre warmed with raw umber), ivory
output.composition: Scale placed left of center, tilted subtly on its axis, occupying roughly a third of the canvas height. The right two-thirds and upper half breathe as open cream paper. The heavier pan drops toward the lower-left, giving the composition quiet tension without symmetry.
output.watercolor_treatment: Cool Payne's grey wet-on-wet wash builds the shadowed underside of the scale arms and base, pooling darker at the fulcrum and diluting almost to nothing at the edges. Paper grain dominates the negative space where pigment is near-absent. The brass pans receive a single thin wash of yellow ochre deepened with raw umber, applied dry-brush so the cold-press texture scuffs through. One or two deepening grey washes at the pivot point only. Pencil under-drawing faintly visible along the chain lines.
output.prompt: A single oxidized brass balance scale rests left of center on warm cream cold-press paper, one pan hanging fractionally lower than the other, the asymmetry quiet and deliberate. The scale occupies roughly a third of the composition's height; the remaining canvas breathes as near-bare paper, grain visible where pigment thins to nothing. Payne's grey builds the shadow beneath the arms and base in a wet-on-wet wash that pools darkest at the fulcrum and bleeds outward until almost absent. The pans receive one dry-brush pass of yellow ochre warmed with raw umber, the cold-press texture scuffing through the pigment. Two deepening grey washes concentrate weight only at the pivot. Pencil under-drawing shows faintly along the hanging chains. No hard edges, no outlines — forms bleed softly into the ivory ground.

output.reasoning: Dominant issue is palette drift: the paper color is pushed warmer than the series anchor’s ivory/cool-neutral ground, risking a warmer cover than the lamplit but restrained neutral system allows.

The single issue flagged is high severity and clearly valid: 'warm cream' drifts the paper ground warmer than the series anchor specifies (ivory, cool neutrals). Replacing it with 'ivory' is a precise, surgical fix that brings the prompt back into alignment without changing anything else. No over-correction detected.

decisions.reason: 'Warm cream' introduces palette drift away from the series anchor's ivory/cool-neutral ground; replacing with 'ivory' restores fidelity to the established system.

output.final_prompt: A single oxidized brass balance scale rests left of center on ivory cold-press paper, one pan hanging fractionally lower than the other, the asymmetry quiet and deliberate. The scale occupies roughly a third of the composition's height; the remaining canvas breathes as near-bare paper, grain visible where pigment thins to nothing. Payne's grey builds the shadow beneath the arms and base in a wet-on-wet wash that pools darkest at the fulcrum and bleeds outward until almost absent. The pans receive one dry-brush pass of yellow ochre warmed with raw umber, the cold-press texture scuffing through the pigment. Two deepening grey washes concentrate weight only at the pivot. Pencil under-drawing shows faintly along the hanging chains. No hard edges, no outlines — forms bleed softly into the ivory ground.
output.changes_summary: 'Warm cream cold-press paper' was replaced with 'ivory cold-press paper' to eliminate palette drift and align the paper ground with the series anchor's cool-neutral ivory system. No other changes were made.

final_prompt: A single oxidized brass balance scale rests left of center on ivory cold-press paper, one pan hanging fractionally lower than the other, the asymmetry quiet and deliberate. The scale occupies roughly a third of the composition's height; the remaining canvas breathes as near-bare paper, grain visible where pigment thins to nothing. Payne's grey builds the shadow beneath the arms and base in a wet-on-wet wash that pools darkest at the fulcrum and bleeds outward until almost absent. The pans receive one dry-brush pass of yellow ochre warmed with raw umber, the cold-press texture scuffing through the pigment. Two deepening grey washes concentrate weight only at the pivot.
